Adoption is the name of the game in business travel, because there's so many reasons for you as a user to book outside of the platform, and that the less you remove those reasons, the more people are in the tool, the more the bookings happen within your policy, the more you have visibility over what's happening, and then you can optimize your program.
It is common for us to go and meet very large global companies, and their program adoption, the number of people that bookings happen in the tool, is below 50%.

Anyway, there's a lot of types of sources we can connect to, to fetch content.
Many booking tools tend to rely on a single source of content, which is a bit old school, which is called a GDS, or Global Distribution System, which works well but actually has a lot of limitations.
We in the industry are quite, I would say, very innovative in the way we go connect to different types of sources, and this gives you two things.

This gives you access to more options, because some sources have limited options, and this also gives you access to more competitive prices, because you might find the same flight in three different sources, and what we do is we compare all those, and we give you the cheapest one, all right?

So here, we drive more options, which is great for adoption, people finding what they need in the tool, but also more savings, because then we give you the most competitive out of the three, and very often, you're tied to just one single source in the industry, which causes a lot of frustrations.

Any flight above $2,000, I want to approve.

The dynamic threshold is a bit easier because it changes based on market at the time.
